objectives: Explain core TCP/IP concepts and common network protocols used in enterprise environments
Interpret basic protocol behaviors to support security investigations
learning_path.title: TCP/IP Foundations for Security Work

objectives: Describe common network security controls and how they reduce risk
Relate network security concepts to real-world attack paths and defensive strategies
learning_path.title: Network Security Essentials

objectives: Identify key fields and patterns in security-relevant logs
Perform basic log triage to separate normal activity from suspicious activity
learning_path.title: Log Analysis Fundamentals

objectives: Explain how a SIEM supports security monitoring workflows
Use Security Monitoring And Siem (Splunk/Elastic) to search and filter events for investigation
learning_path.title: Security Monitoring and SIEM Basics (Splunk/Elastic)

objectives: Apply a structured approach to evaluate suspicious activity
Document findings and confidence levels during analysis
learning_path.title: Threat Detection and Analysis Workflow

objectives: Describe phases of Incident Response and expected outputs
Run a basic Incident Response process from detection through closure
learning_path.title: Incident Response Lifecycle and Execution

objectives: Explain vulnerability management stages from identification to remediation verification
Prioritize vulnerabilities using risk-based reasoning
learning_path.title: Vulnerability Management Program Basics

objectives: Explain core Windows security concepts relevant to enterprise environments
Describe common Windows Security And Active Directory security issues and investigation starting points
learning_path.title: Windows Security and Active Directory Fundamentals

objectives: Explain core Linux security concepts and common misconfigurations
Identify Linux security-relevant artifacts useful for investigations
learning_path.title: Linux Security Fundamentals

objectives: Explain how Endpoint Detection And Response (Edr) supports detection and response
Use Endpoint Detection And Response (Edr) outputs to support investigations and response actions
learning_path.title: Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) Concepts and Use

objectives: Operate open source software to support security monitoring and analysis tasks
Evaluate basic operational considerations when using open source software in workflows
learning_path.title: Operate Open Source Software for Security Tasks

objectives: Use an application-specific interface to complete common operational tasks efficiently
Navigate and apply interface features to support monitoring and investigation workflows
learning_path.title: Use an Application-Specific Interface for Security Operations

objectives: Apply scientific research methodology to form hypotheses and test them using available evidence
Improve investigation quality by documenting methods, assumptions, and limitations
learning_path.title: Scientific Research Methodology for Security Investigations

objectives: Use data science concepts to summarize and explore security-relevant datasets
Apply basic analytical reasoning to support detection and investigation decisions
learning_path.title: Data Science for Security Analytics

objectives: Create a structured plan to manage personal professional development aligned to security responsibilities
Track progress and adjust learning goals based on outcomes and feedback
learning_path.title: Manage Personal Professional Development for Security Roles

matched_occupation.description: Computer scientists conduct research in computer and information science, directed toward greater knowledge and understanding of fundamental aspects of ICT phenomena. They write research reports and proposals. Computer scientists also invent and design new approaches to computing technology, find innovative uses for existing technology and studies and solve complex problems in computing.
matched_occupation.label: computer scientist
